article_meta.title: PoliticsMeet the New Proud BoysThe far-right group's views and tactics are now emulated by federal agents.By Ali BrelandIllustration by The Atlantic

reasoning: The model correctly identifies genuine bias instances in the author's own prose without flagging quoted text. All five flagged items come from the author's connecting prose and editorial framing, not from quoted sources. The bias type labels are reasonable and defensible. The model avoids attribution rule violations - none of the flagged text is inside quotation marks or attributed to sources. The identified instances are genuine examples of author-level bias in an opinion/analysis piece.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All five flagged instances are from the author's own prose. No quoted text from Tarrio, McLaughlin, Jackson, Westwood, or any other source was flagged. The model correctly adhered to the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All five flagged items are defensible as genuine author-level bias. The 'in-house militia' characterization, 'catastrophic results,' 'relentless intimidation,' 'white-supremacist propaganda,' and 'extreme far-right ethos' are all the author's own loaded framing choices. Perhaps the 'extreme far-right' one is slightly weaker since it could be argued as descriptive, but it's still a defensible flag.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model caught the major instances. It could have also flagged 'wanton actions by the state' as loaded word choice, or the overall framing structure that draws sustained parallels between federal agents and extremist militias. The headline framing 'Meet the New Proud Boys' could also be flagged. But the major instances were captured.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The labels are reasonable. 'Opinion Statements Presented as Fact' for the militia claim is correct. 'Sensationalism/Emotionalism' for 'catastrophic results' is appropriate. 'Slant' for the combined framing is defensible. 'Unsubstantiated Claims' for the white-supremacist propaganda assertion is accurate. 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives' for 'extreme far-right ethos' works well.
